title: Slow-Cooked Polish Roast Casserole
description: A hearty, comforting dish featuring beef roast, potatoes, and a hint of red chilli flakes. This Polish-inspired casserole is easy to prepare and perfect for a lazy Sunday.
instructions: Season the beef roast with salt and pepper.
In a large Dutch oven, brown the beef roast on all sides over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion, sliced carrots, minced garlic, and red chilli flakes to the pot and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Add the tomato paste, beef broth, thyme, and parsley to the pot, stirring to combine.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise the casserole for 2 1/2 hours, or until the beef is tender.
While the casserole is cooking, peel and cube the potatoes and set them aside.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in the cubed potatoes.
Return the pot to the oven and cook for an additional 30 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving.
Using two forks, shred the beef into bite-sized pieces and stir them back into the casserole.
Serve the casserole hot, garnished with fresh parsley if desired.
